payload.token: ify the schema of the relevant tables and validate the SQL queries before building the app.

payload.input: {"database":"somerville","sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='main_gold' AND table_name IN ('fct_311_requests','dim_request_type','dim_status','dim_ward','dim_date') ORDER BY table_name, ordinal_position"}
payload.name: execute_sql

payload.input: {"database":"somerville","sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='main_admin' AND table_name='fct_pipeline_run_raw' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}
payload.name: execute_sql

payload.token: ` columns directly (so I don't strictly need to join the dims, but the user wants me to join them —
specifically dim_ward for `ward_name` + `area_sqkm`, dim_date for `year`). Let me verify each

payload.input: {"database":"somerville","sql":"WITH rats AS (SELECT f.* FROM main_gold.fct_311_requests f JOIN main_gold.dim_request_type rt ON f.request_type_id = rt.request_type_id WHERE TRIM(rt.request_type) IN ('Rats','Rodent Assistance Program request','Rodent Assistance Program inquiry','School Rodent Control')) SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_rat_complaints FROM rats"}
payload.name: execute_sql

payload.input: {"database":"somerville","sql":"WITH rats AS (SELECT f.* FROM main_gold.fct_311_requests f JOIN main_gold.dim_request_type rt ON f.request_type_id = rt.request_type_id WHERE TRIM(rt.request_type) IN ('Rats','Rodent Assistance Program request','Rodent Assistance Program inquiry','School Rodent Control')), closed AS (SELECT r.*, (r.most_recent_status_dt - r.date_created_dt) AS days_to_close FROM rats r JOIN main_gold.dim_status s ON r.status_id = s.status_id WHERE r.most_recent_status_dt IS NOT NULL AND s.status = 'Closed') SELECT CASE WHEN days_to_close < 1 THEN '0 days' WHEN days_to_close < 4 THEN '1-3 days' WHEN days_to_close < 8 THEN '4-7 days' WHEN days_to_close < 31 THEN '8-30 days' WHEN days_to_close < 91 THEN '31-90 days' WHEN days_to_close < 366 THEN '91-365 days' ELSE '365+ days' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS complaints FROM closed GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY MIN(days_to_close)"}
payload.name: execute_sql

payload.input: {"database":"somerville","sql":"WITH rats AS (SELECT f.* FROM main_gold.fct_311_requests f JOIN main_gold.dim_request_type rt ON f.request_type_id = rt.request_type_id WHERE TRIM(rt.request_type) IN ('Rats','Rodent Assistance Program request','Rodent Assistance Program inquiry','School Rodent Control')) SELECT d.year, COUNT(*) AS complaints FROM rats r JOIN main_gold.dim_date d ON r.date_created_dt = d.date_dt WHERE d.year BETWEEN 2015 AND 2026 GROUP BY d.year ORDER BY d.year"}
payload.name: execute_sql

payload.input: {"database":"somerville","sql":"WITH rats AS (SELECT f.* FROM main_gold.fct_311_requests f JOIN main_gold.dim_request_type rt ON f.request_type_id = rt.request_type_id WHERE TRIM(rt.request_type) IN ('Rats','Rodent Assistance Program request','Rodent Assistance Program inquiry','School Rodent Control')) SELECT w.ward_name, w.area_sqkm, COUNT(*) AS complaints, ROUND(COUNT(*) / w.area_sqkm, 1) AS complaints_per_sqkm FROM rats r JOIN main_gold.dim_ward w ON r.ward = w.ward WHERE r.ward IS NOT NULL GROUP BY w.ward_name, w.area_sqkm ORDER BY w.ward_name"}
payload.name: execute_sql

payload.input: {"database":"somerville","sql":"SELECT MAX(run_completed_at) AS last_refreshed FROM main_admin.fct_pipeline_run_raw WHERE run_status='success'"}
payload.name: execute_sql

payload.token: four queries work and numbers match the brief (14,036 total, 7 wards,
167 NULL-ward implied by 14,036 - 13,869 = 167). Now let me build the app

questions.prompt: The builder ran out of token budget (4096 tokens). Continue with double the budget (8192 tokens)?
